title: Bridge Essay: Canonical neural networks perform active inference.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent-arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Dependent arising — predictive processing, free energy principle, active inference, Bayesian brain, prediction error in perception and action.

**Canonical neural networks perform active inference.** — Commun Biol. PMID: 35031656. DOI: 10.1038/s42003-021-02994-2. This work considers aclass of canonical neural networks comprising rate coding models, wherein neural activity and plasticity minimise acommon cost function-and plasticity is modulated with acertain delay. We show that such neural networks implicitly perform active inference and learning to minimise the risk associated with future outcomes.
